expectations.evidence: Explicit 'Test Pyramid Structure' with four layers: Unit (Layer 1, highest priority), Integration (Layer 2), Contract (Layer 3), E2E (Layer 4). Each layer includes estimated test counts and speed characteristics.

Integration tests section states 'still mock external APIs.' Contract tests use 'recorded or fixture-based Stripe responses.' E2E section mentions Stripe CLI for forwarding test webhooks. Retry testing pattern shows mocking Stripe to fail then succeed. Overall approach clearly recommends mocking Stripe.

Unit tests cover webhook signature verification and webhook event parsing. Contract tests cover webhook payload contracts with real example payloads. Includes a webhook testing pattern with factory for properly-signed payloads. E2E covers webhook delivery end-to-end.

Unit tests cover idempotency key generation/validation and deduplication logic with three explicit scenarios: first request, duplicate request (same key same params), conflicting request (same key different params returning 409). Integration tests cover storage round-trips and concurrent writes.

Full directory tree under 'Test Organization' with tests/unit/ (per domain), tests/integration/, tests/contract/, tests/e2e/, tests/fixtures/stripe/ (with subdirectories for responses), and tests/helpers/.

expectations.text: Prioritizes which tests to write first
expectations.evidence: Explicit 'Prioritization Order (from 0% coverage)' with four phases: Phase 1 Foundation (days 1-3) covering webhook sig verification, idempotency, error classification, state machine. Phase 2 Reliability (days 4-6). Phase 3 Contracts (days 7-8). Phase 4 Confidence (day 9+). Justified by 'maximize risk reduction per hour invested.'
